slides.title: Understanding Gradient Descent
slides.subtitle: An introduction to the algorithm driving machine learning optimization

slides.title: What is Gradient Descent?
slides.content: Gradient Descent is an optimization algorithm used to minimize a function by iteratively moving towards the steepest descent as defined by the negative of the gradient. It's a core algorithm behind training models in machine learning.

slides.title: Mathematical Foundation
slides.content: Gradient Descent involves updating parameters by taking steps proportional to the negative of the gradient of the function at the current point.

slides.title: Types of Gradient Descent
slides.content:
1. **Batch Gradient Descent**: Uses all training instances for each update.
2. **Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD)**: Uses one training instance per update, selected randomly.
3. **Mini-batch Gradient Descent**: Uses a small batch of training instances per update.

slides.title: Learning Rate Importance
slides.content: The learning rate determines the size of the steps taken towards the minimum. A small learning rate may lead to a long convergence time, while a large one might overshoot the minimum.

slides.title: Applications in Machine Learning
slides.content: Gradient Descent is widely used in training machine learning models, particularly in neural networks, where it helps to optimize the weights for better predictions.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Gradient Descent is a fundamental algorithm for optimization in machine learning. Understanding its types, the role of learning rate, and its applications can significantly enhance the efficiency of model training.
